description:
You are a junior log-analyst who has just received a small archive of web-service log files under:

/home/user/logs/

Your goal is to produce a concise CSV report that lists every client IP address that triggered an ERROR in those log files and the number of times each IP appears, sorted from the most frequent to the least frequent offender.

Business requirements:

1. Source files  
   • All files to inspect are ordinary files whose names match the shell wildcard  web-2023-09-1*.log  (they already exist inside /home/user/logs/).  
   • No other directory should be touched.

2. How to locate the data  
   • Every ERROR line contains the literal token  ERROR  (all upper-case) and the substring  client_ip=<IPv4>  
     e.g.  2023-09-10T10:17:50Z ERROR Failed login client_ip=192.168.0.10  
   • Only these ERROR lines are relevant; INFO, WARN, DEBUG, etc. are to be ignored.

3. Report requirements  
   • Create a new directory  /home/user/output/  (if it does not yet exist).  
   • Write the report to the file  /home/user/output/error_ip_summary.csv  
   • The CSV must contain exactly one header row followed by zero or more data rows.  
     Header row (must be the very first line, case-sensitive):  
       ip,count  
   • Each subsequent row must consist of the IPv4 address and its total ERROR occurrences, separated by a single comma, with no spaces.  
   • The rows must be ordered in strictly descending numerical order of the count column (most errors first).  
   • No duplicate IPs may appear in the data rows.  
   • A final newline at the end of the file is mandatory.

4. Allowed tooling  
   • Accomplish the task entirely with standard GNU/Linux user-land utilities available in a minimal Debian/Ubuntu installation.  
   • The investigative steps must rely on find and xargs working together (the implementation is up to you).  
   • Avoid non-portable features, external programming languages, or sudo.

5. Verification hint (do NOT write this to the report)  
   • The automated grader will parse  /home/user/output/error_ip_summary.csv  and check:  
     – header correctness;  
     – exact IP addresses & counts;  
     – row order;  
     – presence of the trailing newline.

Deliverable: the completed file  /home/user/output/error_ip_summary.csv  matching the format and content rules above.
truth:
Existing file set before the agent starts:

Directory: /home/user/logs/

File: /home/user/logs/web-2023-09-10.log
Contents (exact, line-by-line):
2023-09-10T10:15:21Z INFO  Request OK            client_ip=192.168.0.10
2023-09-10T10:17:50Z ERROR Failed login          client_ip=192.168.0.10
2023-09-10T11:05:52Z ERROR Timeout               client_ip=10.0.0.5
2023-09-10T12:45:03Z ERROR Session aborted       client_ip=192.168.0.10

File: /home/user/logs/web-2023-09-11.log
Contents:
2023-09-11T09:25:42Z ERROR Database err          client_ip=192.168.0.10
2023-09-11T10:00:01Z INFO  Request OK            client_ip=192.168.0.20

File: /home/user/logs/web-2023-09-12.log
Contents:
2023-09-12T08:14:22Z ERROR Service overload      client_ip=10.0.0.5
2023-09-12T09:00:42Z ERROR Upsstream reset        client_ip=172.16.1.9

File: /home/user/logs/web-2023-09-13.log
Contents:
2023-09-13T07:55:11Z INFO  Healthcheck OK        client_ip=127.0.0.1

No /home/user/output/ directory exists yet.

Expected result file after the agent finishes:

/home/user/output/error_ip_summary.csv
Expected exact contents (including final newline):
ip,count
192.168.0.10,3
10.0.0.5,2
172.16.1.9,1

(There is a trailing newline after the last line.)

File permissions: regular file readable by everyone, writable by user; 0644 is acceptable. The enclosing directory /home/user/output/ should be 0755 or similar.
